text:
Once upon a time, in a forest filled with dappled sunlight filtering through tall trees, there lived a fox named Fredrick. Fredrick loved exploring the forest floor, hunting for berries, and napping in cool hollows. Life was simple, but one day a wise old owl named Oliver stopped him mid-snapberry.

Oliver hooted softly, "Fredrick, young fox, you've caught countless mice. Why not share some?"

Fredrick blinked in surprise. "Share?" he crooned.

"Yes," Oliver said. "Sharing makes your forest your home instead of just your dinner. Look how many you have."

Fredrick counted thirty. Feeling generous, he decided to release the others. That afternoon, Fredrick spotted a small kit that looked lost. It wouldn't let him touch its paw.

"Perhaps you'd care? I wouldn't take it." Fredrick held out his paw slowly.
